channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The eye-popping price of Harry Styles tickets has social media users furious
item.description: More than 11.5 million fans signed up for presale tickets to Harry Styles’s upcoming Madison Square Garden residency for the Together, Together tour. But when tickets went on sale January 26, amid the excitement, many fans were left frustrated by lengthy virtual queue waits.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: This very expensive fleece is making waves for looking like startup swag
item.description: How much would you pay for a gray fleece? Yes, the type that’s ubiquitous in corporate cubicles and business-casual work conferences across America. What if it had the Miu Miu logo stitched on the left chest? If you said $2,500, you’d be on the money.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: This whole AI thing is simpler than you think
item.description: At least three-quarters of the speaking invitations I get these days are about AI. But lately, they’re for different reasons. Companies used to bring me in under the assumption that artificial intelligence was going to change everything.
item.dc:creator: Douglas Rushkoff, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon cuts thousands of roles
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google expands BigQuery with conversational agent and custom agent tools
item.description: Google has added Conversational Analytics to its BigQuery data warehouse, which it says will allow enterprise data teams and business users to ask questions about data in natural language, in turn speeding up data analytics for AI use cases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Waymo probed after robotaxi hit child near California elementary school
item.description: While police characterized the incident as a “non-injury collision,” Waymo told regulators that the child sustained minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: When government stops looking ahead, risks multiply: New survey shows foresight fading fast
item.description: "I think this is really an essential next step as we look to continuing the transformation that's going on in the government right now," Cunzeman said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: New PDF compression filter will save space, need software updates
item.description: Brotli is one of the most widely used but least-known compression formats ever devised, long incorporated into all major browsers and web content delivery networks (CDNs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Humanoid robot makes architectural history by designing a building
item.description: Ai-Da Robot became the first humanoid robot to design a building with a modular Space Pod concept for Moon and Mars bases, featured at the Utzon Center in Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why a lack of governance will hurt companies using agentic AI
item.description: Businesses are acting fast to adopt agentic AI—artificial intelligence systems that work without human guidance—but have been much slower to put governance in place to oversee them, a new survey shows. That mismatch is a major source of risk in AI adoption.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: From Meta to Samsung, global tech unleashes spending to chase AI
item.description: The world's largest tech firms show no signs of easing up on AI spending, a record wave that's propelling hardware providers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Silicon Valley is raising billions while San Diego biotechs struggle
item.description: Venture capitalists are funding startups building AI. Meanwhile, life sciences venture capital in San Diego has dried up, leading to leaner firms and slower job growth.
item.dc:creator: Noelle Harff, The Mercury News

item.title: China clears Nvidia H200 sales to tech giants, reshaping AI data center plans
item.description: China has begun cautiously reopening access to Nvidia’s advanced AI processors, granting approvals to a small group of its largest technology companies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is prompt engineering? The art of AI orchestration
item.description: Prompt engineering is the process of crafting inputs, or prompts, to a generative AI system that lead to the system producing better outputs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why your next microservices should be streaming SQL-driven
item.description: “It is tempting, if the only tool you have is a hammer, to treat everything as if it were a nail.” — Abraham Maslow, 1966 Microservices provide many benefits for building business services, such as independent tools, technologies, languages, release cycles, and full control over your dependencies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google’s LiteRT adds advanced hardware acceleration
item.description: LiteRT, Google’s “modern” on-device inference framework evolved from TensorFlow Lite (TFLite), has introduced advanced acceleration capabilities, based on a ”next-generation GPU engine” called ML Drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World
